Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

1238	  Let \(f : X \to Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes. Then the pullback functor on
1239	  \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is
1240	  one half of an equivalence of categories

1247	  \((f^{-1})^*\). The unit and counit isomorphisms are built from the pseudofunctor
1248	  coherences of pullback: \((f^{-1})^* \circ f^* \cong (f \circ f^{-1})^* = \mathrm{id}^*
1249	  \cong \mathrm{id}\) and symmetrically, using
1250	  \(f \circ f^{-1} = \mathrm{id}_Y\), \(f^{-1} \circ f = \mathrm{id}_X\)
1251	  (composition-of-pullbacks, congruence under equal maps, and pullback-of-identity).
1252	  The triangle identities are forced by these coherences.

% --- Seam 2, step (iii): the mate-unwinding crux, cut into five atomic links. ---
% These five lemmas decompose the surviving step-(iii) obligation of
% lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs (the unmoved "mate-unwinding crux") into one
% mathematical move each. They are Archon-original adjoint-mate calculus over the proved
% change-of-rings dictionaries; there is no external source (no % SOURCE lines). Composites
% are read left to right, as elsewhere in this chapter.

\begin{lemma}
[(iii-1) Unit expansion: inverting the comp-coherence]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand}
  \uses{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
  Let \(e : X' \xrightarrow{\sim} \operatorname{Spec}(A \otimes_R R')\) be the canonical
  isomorphism, \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) the left tensor inclusion, and set
  \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\). For an \(A\)-module \(M\), the bare \((g')\)-unit
  \(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\) of the \((g')\)-adjunction expands as the four-factor composite
  \[
    \eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}
      \;=\;
      \eta^{\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}_{\widetilde M}
      \,\circ\, (\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\bigl(\eta^{e}_{(\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)^{*}\widetilde M}\bigr)
      \,\circ\, (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}_{\widetilde M}
      \,\circ\, (g')_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}_{\widetilde M}\bigr),
  \]
  where composites are read left to right.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
  The leg-reindex engine Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}, instantiated at \(a = e\),
  \(b = \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), \(N = \widetilde M\), reads
  \[
    \eta^{\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}_{\widetilde M}
      \circ (\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}(\eta^{e})
      \circ (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}
    \;=\;
    \eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}
      \circ (g')_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{inv}}\bigr).
  \]
  Post-compose both sides with \((g')_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}\bigr)\).
  Since \((g')_{*}\) is a functor it carries
  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp})^{\mathrm{inv}} \circ (\mathrm{pullbackComp})^{\mathrm{hom}} = \mathrm{id}\)
  to the identity, so the right-hand side collapses to \(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\), giving the stated
  expansion.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[(iii-2) Distribute the expansion through $(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*$ and $\Gamma$]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand}
  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand}, applying the
  functor \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\) and then global sections \(\Gamma = \Gamma_R\) to the
  expanded unit distributes over the four factors:
  \[
    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M})\bigr)
    \;=\;
    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\eta^{\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}_{\widetilde M}\bigr)
    \,\circ\,
    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}(\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\eta^{e}\bigr)
    \,\circ\,
    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}\bigr)
    \,\circ\,
    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}(g')_{*}(\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}\bigr).
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand}
  Rewrite \(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\) by the expansion of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand}. Both \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\)
  and \(\Gamma\) are functors, so each preserves the four-fold composite
  (\(F(u \circ v) = F u \circ F v\)). No further content.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[(iii-3) $e$-factor cancellation against the codomain read]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_eCancel}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_eCancel}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute,
        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs, lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso,
        lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id}
  Notation as in Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute}, with
  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\) and \(Z = f'_*(g')^{*}\widetilde M\). Write
  \(\gamma_\psi\) for the comparison \(\Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\psi)_* Z\bigr) \cong
  \operatorname{restr}_\psi \Gamma_{R'}(Z)\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}) and
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}^{\flat}\) for the variable-legs codomain read
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs}). The codomain read
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}^{\flat}\) is, by construction, a composite that contains exactly three
  \(e\)-pieces: the pullback comparison
  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) (inside its
  \(\mathrm{iso}_{g}\) factor), the inverse \(e\)-pushforward unit isomorphism
  \((\eta^{e})^{-1}\) (inside its \(\mathrm{unit\_iso}^{-1}\) factor, an isomorphism because \(e\) is an
  isomorphism, Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}), and
  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}})^{\mathrm{inv}}\).
  Then, on global sections over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), the three \(e\)-dependent factors produced
  by step~(ii)+(iii-2) — the image of the \(e\)-unit
  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\eta^{e}\), the trailing
  \((g')_{*}(\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}\), and the surviving
  step-(ii) coherence \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',\operatorname{Spec}\varphi})^{\mathrm{hom}}\)
  (whose \(\Gamma\)-image is the identity, Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id}) —
  cancel against those three \(e\)-pieces of \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}^{\flat}\). Concretely, the whole
  section-level left-hand side collapses to the affine remnant
  \[

      \circ \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M})\bigr)
      \circ \Gamma\bigl((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',\operatorname{Spec}\varphi})^{\mathrm{hom}}\bigr)

  \(e\) left. This is the load-bearing telescoping of the whole reindex.

  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}); the trailing
  \((g')_{*}(\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) cancels the
  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) inside \(\mathrm{iso}_g\);
  and the step-(ii) coherence \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',\operatorname{Spec}\varphi})^{\mathrm{hom}}\),
  whose \(\Gamma\)-image is the identity
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id}), together with
  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) re-associates the
  iterated pushforward along the two factorizations of the square's composite and disappears on
  sections. The three cancellations leave the affine remnant
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}^{\mathrm{aff}}\) and the affine unit factor as displayed.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[(iii-4) Affine-unit value (Seam 1 application)]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_affineUnit}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_affineUnit}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso,

  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_eCancel}, the surviving
  affine factor — the \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit \(\eta^{\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}_{\widetilde M}\)
  pushed forward by \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\), read on global sections over
  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) through the source dictionary \(\gamma^{\mathrm{src}}\) and the affine
  codomain remnant \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}^{\mathrm{aff}}\) — equals the restriction of scalars along
  \(\varphi\) of the algebraic unit \(\eta_M\) of the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction
  along \(\iota_A\):
  \[

      \circ \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\eta^{\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}_{\widetilde M}\bigr)
      \circ \bigl(\text{affine tilde dictionaries}\bigr)
    \;=\;

  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_eCancel}
  Seam~1 (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}) computes the \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit
  read over \(\operatorname{Spec} A\) through the tilde dictionaries: it is exactly the algebraic unit

  \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\) and read over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) rather than over

  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso} identifies

  \(\operatorname{restr}_{\varphi}\) of its \(\Gamma_A\)-value. Applying it to Seam~1's value gives
  \(\operatorname{restr}_{\varphi}(\eta_M)\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[(iii-5) Match the inner value]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_innerMatch}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_innerMatch}
  \uses{def:base_change_mate_inner_value, lem:base_change_mate_unit_value,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_affineUnit}

  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_affineUnit} over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by
  restriction of scalars along \(\psi\), and transporting across the ring equation
  \(\iota_A \circ \varphi = \iota_{R'} \circ \psi\), yields exactly the inner comparison value
  \(\rho\) of Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}:
  \[
    \rho \;=\; \operatorname{restr}_{\psi}\Bigl(\text{transport of }\operatorname{restr}_{\varphi}(\eta_M)
      \text{ across } \iota_A \circ \varphi = \iota_{R'} \circ \psi\Bigr),
    \qquad \rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1)\otimes m .
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:base_change_mate_inner_value, lem:base_change_mate_unit_value,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_affineUnit}
  This is the definitional unfolding of \(\rho\). By
  Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}, \(\rho\) is built precisely as the restriction of
  scalars along \(\varphi\) of the algebraic unit \(\eta_M\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}), transported across the ring equation
  \(\iota_A \circ \varphi = \iota_{R'} \circ \psi\) by the change-of-rings comparison
  \(\operatorname{restr}_{\varphi \cdot \iota_A} = \operatorname{restr}_{\psi} \circ \operatorname{restr}_{\iota_{R'}}\).
  Hence the value produced by Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_affineUnit}, read over
  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by \(\operatorname{restr}_{\psi}\) along that equation, is by construction

\end{proof}
